title: Soldier Shadow Drill Commander
description: A soldier performs animation clips on a shadow-casting platform. The user controls animation playback, blending, speed, shadow intensity, and camera orbit to choreograph a military drill sequence. Each parameter change is validated and reflected in real-time with smooth transitions.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads both ShadowmappableMesh.glb and soldier.glb using GLTFLoader. Place the ShadowmappableMesh as a ground platform at y=0 centered at the origin. Place the soldier on top of the platform facing forward. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 2, 5) looking at the soldier. Add a DirectionalLight at (5, 10, 7) with castShadow enabled, shadow mapSize 2048x2048, and configure the shadow camera frustum to cover the platform. Enable shadowMap on the renderer with PCFSoftShadowMap. Both the soldier and platform must cast and receive shadows. Extract all AnimationClips from the soldier model and create an AnimationMixer. Build an HTML control panel (fixed, top-left, styled with semi-transparent dark background and white text) with these interactive controls: (1) A dropdown to select the active animation clip from all available clips, defaulting to the first clip. (2) A slider for animation speed (timeScale) ranging from 0.1 to 3.0, default 1.0, with a numeric label showing the current value. (3) A slider for crossfade duration (0.1 to 2.0 seconds, default 0.5) used when switching clips. (4) A slider for shadow darkness/intensity on the DirectionalLight (0.0 to 1.0, default 1.0). (5) A checkbox to toggle shadow visibility on the platform mesh. (6) A button labeled 'Reset Pose' that crossfades back to the first clip at default speed. When the user selects a new animation clip, crossfade from the current action to the new one using the crossfade duration value. Validate all numeric inputs: clamp speed between 0.1 and 3.0, clamp crossfade between 0.1 and 2.0, clamp shadow intensity between 0.0 and 1.0. Add OrbitControls from three/addons targeting the soldier center (0, 1, 0) with damping enabled, min distance 2, max distance 12. The render loop must call mixer.update(delta) each frame using a Clock. Set renderer background to color 0x1a1a2e with tone mapping set to ACESFilmicToneMapping. Ensure the scene resizes properly on window resize.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  activeClipIndex: number,         // index of the currently playing animation clip (0-based)
  animationSpeed: number,         // timeScale multiplier for animation playback (0.1 to 3.0)
  crossfadeDuration: number,         // duration in seconds for blending between animation clips (0.1 to 2.0)
  shadowIntensity: number,         // opacity/intensity of shadows (0.0 to 1.0)
  cameraOrbitAngle: number,         // horizontal rotation angle of camera around soldier in radians (0 to 2π)
  isAnimationPlaying: boolean,         // whether the animation mixer is actively playing
  totalClipsAvailable: number,         // total count of animation clips loaded from soldier model
  currentMixerTime: number,         // current playback time of the active animation in seconds
  platformShadowsEnabled: boolean,         // whether the platform is receiving shadows
  lightPositionX: number,         // x-coordinate of the directional light position
  blendWeight: number,         // current blend weight during crossfade transition (0.0 to 1.0)
}
